claim: Testing your prompts and LLMs across local and cloud providers using tools like PromptFoo reveals readiness for production use cases
actionable: Set up PromptFoo test suites that run the same prompts against both cloud and local models to track when local models become viable
source_title: Local - ON DEVICE LLMs are the future, but NOT yet - Here's why

claim: Local LLMs are not production-ready yet - they require significant setup effort, RAM, GPU, and show lower accuracy than cloud providers like GPT-4
actionable: Continue using cloud providers (GPT-4, GPT-3.5) for production until local models reach parity; use local models for offline prototyping
source_title: Local - ON DEVICE LLMs are the future, but NOT yet - Here's why

claim: LLMs are non-deterministic even with temperature at zero - use PromptFoo's repeat feature to test probability of getting correct results
actionable: Run prompts multiple times (e.g., repeat: 5) with your model to calculate success probability rather than relying on single attempts
source_title: Local - ON DEVICE LLMs are the future, but NOT yet - Here's why

claim: LLamaFile enables running entire open-source LLM models from a single executable file without complex setup
actionable: Use LLamaFile to quickly spin up local models for testing rather than managing complex docker/environment setups
source_title: Local - ON DEVICE LLMs are the future, but NOT yet - Here's why

claim: Mistral 7B shows the most promise among open-source models tested against cloud providers, achieving 60% success on SQL generation
actionable: When evaluating local models, benchmark Mistral 7B first as a reference point for open-source capability
source_title: Local - ON DEVICE LLMs are the future, but NOT yet - Here's why

claim: Running local models is resource-intensive - an M2 MacBook Pro with 64GB RAM requires significant compute allocation even for smaller models
actionable: When deploying local models, budget for substantial RAM and GPU resources; test on your target hardware early
source_title: Local - ON DEVICE LLMs are the future, but NOT yet - Here's why

claim: Use PromptFoo with custom model classes to test arbitrary code and LLMs including local models in your test framework
actionable: Create custom PromptFoo classes extending the provider base that wrap your local models to run in the same evaluation framework
source_title: Local - ON DEVICE LLMs are the future, but NOT yet - Here's why
